codeReview.summary: SearchTool implementation verified. The feature was already implemented by a prior worker - this worker correctly identified and verified the existing implementation. All verification steps passed: build, tests, clippy, and workspace tests. The commit 491e98c contains a minor fix (removing unused `mut` in test) unrelated to the core feature.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature specifies skillName 'Rust' but worker reported 'rust-worker' skill not found. The actual skill directory is 'rust' (lowercase, no suffix). The mismatch between feature.skillName ('Rust') and available skills (rust) caused confusion. AGENTS.md and the skill directory both use 'rust' as the slug.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json has skillName: 'Rust' but ~/.factory/skills/ contains 'rust' not 'Rust' or 'rust-worker'. Transcript shows: 'The rust-worker skill specified in the feature did not exist.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed an appropriate deviation when the specified skill was unavailable - they explored the codebase directly and found the implementation already complete. This was a reasonable workaround.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: skillFeedback.deviations: { step: '1.1 Read Context + Invoke Worker Skill', whatIDidInstead: 'The rust-worker skill specified in the feature did not exist. Instead, I explored the codebase...', why: 'Skill not found - feature implementation was already complete' }

summary: Feature tools-search passes review. SearchTool (grep, glob, symbol_search) was already implemented in crates/swell-tools/src/tools.rs with adequate test coverage. The worker correctly verified existing implementation rather than duplicating work. Verification commands (cargo build, cargo test -p swell-tools -- search, cargo clippy) all passed. Minor observation: skill name mismatch between feature specification and available skills - no action required since implementation is complete.
